title: Today's Tech Brief: Hands-On With Gemini Spark
summary: Google's new AI agent Gemini Spark and Liquid AI's 8B-A1B MoE model make headlines

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. Google recently introduced Gemini Spark, an always-on AI agent that connects to your personal data, completes online tasks, and automates daily interactions. I came across a hands-on experience with Gemini Spark that caught my attention.

The Wired article 'Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend' shares an interesting experience. The author gave Gemini Spark access to their life, and it apparently decided to 'friend-zone' their boyfriend. This shows how Gemini Spark can make decisions based on the data it has access to. It's a bit unsettling to think that an AI can have such a personal opinion, but it's also fascinating to see how these agents can learn and adapt.

Next, Liquid AI revealed its LFM2.5-8B-A1B model, an 8 billion parameter Mixture of Experts trained on 38 trillion tokens. This model is optimized for fast and reliable performance on consumer hardware. It's competitive with much larger models, which is impressive. The potential for high-performance AI on everyday devices could change the game for many industries.

Another interesting development is the growing accessibility of AI. With Gemini Spark and Liquid AI's model, we're seeing AI become more mainstream and user-friendly. This could lead to some exciting innovations in the future, from smarter home assistants to more efficient work tools.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend
Liquid AI reveals 8B-A1B MoE trained on 38T
